Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |Alternate name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun |Group= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |Coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |Size=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)

(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |Period=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic site= National [[Historic Site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun

Kurumazuka Kofun |Label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!Shape!!Size!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |Alternate name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun |Group= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |Burial structure= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |Period=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic site= National [[Historic Site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-designated) |Notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!Shape!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun |Group= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |Shape=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |Burial structure= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |Period=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic site= National [[Historic Site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-designated) |Notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!Shape!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Cultural Properties Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto other_designation Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible other_designation of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |Burial structure= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |Period=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic site= National [[Historic Site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-designated) |Notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |Icon= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic Site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !Period !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!Burial structure |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto other_designation Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible other_designation of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label position= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated artifacts have been designated as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible Cultural Property]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National Historic Site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was designated as a [[National other_designation]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its artifacts were designated as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == Artifacts == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{Cultural Property Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property === * Artifacts from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko Cultural Property Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto other_designation Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Category:Kofun in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Muko City History]] [[Category:National other_designations of Japan]] [[Category:Tangible other_designation of Japan]] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January[[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:物集女車塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] List of other_designations of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]
