{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=[[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=[[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=[[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|物集女車塚古墳}}
