revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 物集女車塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
{{small|（右に前方部、左奥に後円部。右端は物集女街道）}} |別名 = 寺戸車塚古墳 |所属 = 乙訓古墳群 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[向日市]][[物集女|物集女町]]南条 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 28.80 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 57.50 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46m（48m）
高さ9m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式家形石棺） |出土品 = 金銅広帯式冠・馬具ほか副葬品多数、[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「物集女車塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財 = 出土品（京都府指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 物集女
車塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''物集女車塚古墳'''（もずめくるまづかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]][[物集女|物集女町]]南条にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。乙訓古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）、出土品は京都府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+国の史跡「乙訓古墳群」13基 !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=2|1||[[五塚原古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長91m|| |- |[[元稲荷古墳]]||[[前方後方墳]]||墳丘長94m||竪穴式石槨 |- |2||[[寺戸大塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長98m||竪穴式石槨2 |- |3||[[長法寺南原古墳]]||前方後方墳||墳丘長60m||竪穴式石槨
石棺系小石室 |- |rowspan=2|4||[[天皇の杜古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m|| |- |[[鳥居前古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長51m||竪穴式石槨 |- |5||[[恵解山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長128m||竪穴式石槨?
副葬品埋納施設 |- |7||南条古墳||[[円墳]]||直径23.5m|| |- |rowspan=4|9||芝古墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長32.7m|| |- |井ノ内車塚古墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長37m|| |- |'''物集女車塚古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長48m||横穴式石室 |- |[[井ノ内稲荷塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長46m||横穴式石室
木棺直葬 |- |11||今里大塚古墳||円墳||直径45m||横穴式石室 |}
[[京都盆地]]南西縁、向日丘陵北東部の緩傾斜面上（標高21メートル）に築造された古墳である。かつて[[淳和天皇]]の霊柩車を埋納したとする伝承があり、「車塚」の古墳名はこれに由来する。現在は東側に[[京都府道・大阪府道67号西京高槻線|府道67号西京高槻線]]（物集女街道）が通り、その敷設の際に前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）以降に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]列のほか、[[葺石]]が認められる。墳丘南側くびれ部には[[造出]]を付す。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる。埋葬施設は後円部における片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。石室全長11メートルを測る大型石室であり、[[ヤマト王権]]下で規格化された「畿内型」石室の特徴を呈する。石室内には組合式家形石棺が据えられ、石棺内外からは広帯式冠・耳環・鈴などの装身具類、鹿角製捩環頭大刀・銀製刀装具・大刀・鉄鉾などの武器類、馬具類、[[須恵器]]などの多量の副葬品が検出されている{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]中葉頃（TK10新相型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]初頭頃まで（TK43古相型式期・TK209型式期）3人の追葬が推測される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。乙訓地域を代表する盟主的首長墓として位置づけられ、規格化された畿内型石室を採用するとともに、質・量とも優れた副葬品を有する点で特筆される古墳になる。当時の[[ヤマト王権]]は[[継体天皇|継体大王]]の時代であり、[[市尾墓山古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などの大臣・大連級古墳の次階層の武人的性格の人物の墓と想定され、継体王権における政治階層の墓制秩序の実態を知るうえで注目されるほか、継体大王が置いたとされる[[弟国宮]]との関連性が指摘される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 古墳域は[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月1日に国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）、出土品は[[2018年]]（平成30年）に京都府指定有形文化財に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎年春にのみ一般公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、『山州名跡志』・『[[山陵志]]』に[[淳和天皇]]の車の埋納塚として記述{{Sfn|物集女車塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 * [[1926年]]（[[昭和]]元年）、道路敷設（現在の[[京都府道・大阪府道67号西京高槻線|府道67号西京高槻線]]）に伴う前方部の一部削平。 * [[1931年]]（昭和6年）、墳丘測量調査：第1次調査（[[梅原末治]]）{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * 昭和50年頃には、前方部の崩壊が約半分まで進行{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）以降、発掘調査（向日市教育委員会）。 ** 第2次調査：墳丘確認調査。 ** 第3次調査：石室内部調査、墳丘くびれ部補足調査。 ** 第4次調査：墳形・造出確認調査。 ** 第5次調査：石室羨道部の解体調査。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）４月14日、京都府指定史跡に指定。 * [[1992年|1992]]-[[1994年]]度（[[平成]]4-6年度）、史跡整備。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 * [[2018年]]（平成30年）3月23日、出土品が京都府指定有形文化財に指定。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|右に後円部、左奥に前方部。}}]] 古墳の規模は次の通り[https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755803803.html 物集女車塚古墳（古墳・遺跡 ）]（向日市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：46メートル（資料によっては48メートル{{Sfn|京都の文化財 第36集|2019}}） * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：24-32メートル ** 高さ：9メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 長さ：18-23メートル ** 幅：39メートル ** 高さ：8メートル 墳丘は左右非対称の前方後円形で、北側では前方部が直線的で前面が大きく開くが、南側では緩くくびれて側面に造出を付す{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。葺石は後円部・くびれ部の墳丘上段裾部にのみ、鉢巻状に施される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。埴輪列は、墳丘平坦面のくびれ部付近・墳頂部、南側造出上にのみ配列されたと見られる。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡る可能性が高く、北側には外堤状施設が存在した可能性がある{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 埋葬施設としては、後円部において片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * 石室全長：約11メートル * 玄室：長さ5.1メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3.0メートル * 羨道：長さ5.8メートル、幅1.2-1.5メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室では、側壁は5段積み、前壁は3段積みで、天井石は4枚。袖部は2段積みである。羨道部の作業単位は、墳丘構築時の第一次墳丘と一致しており、墳丘・石室が連動して構築された様子が示唆される。石室の床面には排水溝が構築される。閉塞は前庭部埋土・閉塞石・集積遺構からなり、完存していたため3回の追葬が確認されている{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 石室内の玄室奥には、凝灰岩製組合式家形石棺が据えられる。石棺は石室主軸に直交し、内法で長辺1.86メートル・短辺0.88メートル・高さ0.74メートルを測る。底石3枚・長側石2枚・短側石2枚・蓋石3枚からなり、長側石以外の各石材の側面には縄掛突起を付す。また石棺の内外面には赤色顔料が施される。この石棺の蓋石上には、凝灰岩7石を組合せた棚状構造物が存在したと見られるほか、玄室床面からは石棺内のものと見られる石枕が出土している{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 現在は、石室のうち羨道部は解体復元されている。 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|玄室の組合式家形石棺 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|石室入り口 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|石室排水溝（上に石室入り口） == 出土品 == === 石室出土 === [[ファイル:物集女車塚古墳出土 玉類・須恵器 (野洲市歴史民俗博物館展示).JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|玉類・須恵器}}{{small|野洲市歴史民俗博物館企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[ファイル:物集女車塚古墳出土 土器.JPG|thumb|300px|right|{{center|須恵器・土師器}}{{small|[[和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 石室内の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|京都の文化財 第36集|2019}}。 ; 装身具類 * 石棺内出土 ** 金銅広帯式冠 1頭分 - 京都府内では唯一の金銅製冠の出土例。 ** ガラス小玉 981点 ** ガラス管玉 3点 ** 銀空玉 49点 * 石棺蓋石上出土 ** ガラス玉 3点 * 石室床面上出土 ** 冠に装着されていたガラス玉 6点 ** 銀耳環 2点 ** 碧玉棗玉 2点 ** 琥珀棗玉 3点 ** ガラス小玉 319点 ** ガラストンボ玉 11点 ** 滑石臼玉 1点 ; 武器類 * 石棺内出土 ** 鉄刀 2片 ** 刀装具類残欠 - 鹿角刀装具2点、金銅三輪玉1点、銀刀装具9点、鉄鞘金具1点など。 * 石室床面出土 ** 環頭大刀飾金具 1点 ** 鉄刀 1口以上 ** 鉄小刀 5口 ** 鉄矛 3口 ** 石突3点 * 鉄鏃 117点以上 - いずれも長頸鏃。4点は石室壁面に挿入（垂幕の懸垂用か）。 ; 工具類 * 石棺内出土 ** 刀子 8点以上 ** 刀子装具 - 鹿角把2点。 ; 馬具類 * 1群：f字形鏡板付轡、剣菱形杏葉3点、形式不明杏葉残欠、鉄環状雲珠、革金具組合式辻金具、鉄環状辻金具、鉄地金銅張鞍金具、鐙金具、青銅馬鐸。 * 2群：形式不明轡、楕円形杏葉、有脚伏鉢形雲珠、有脚伏鉢形辻金具。 * 3群：環状鏡板付轡、環状雲珠、鞍金具、鉄革金具。 ; 土器類 * 須恵器 - 杯蓋16点、杯身2点、無蓋高杯5点、有蓋高杯9点、高杯蓋9点、小型無蓋高杯3点、𤭯5点、広口壺2点、壺3点、壺蓋4点、有蓋脚付壺5点、直口壺7点、横瓶1点、甕1点、器台3点、脚部破片7点など。 * 土師器 - 直口壺1点、広口壺1点。 === 墳丘出土 === [[ファイル:物集女車塚古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|円筒埴輪}}{{small|和歌山県立紀伊風土記の丘企画展示時に撮影。}}]] 墳丘からの出土品は次の通り{{Sfn|京都の文化財 第36集|2019}}。 ; 埴輪 * 埴輪基底部 32個 ** 後円部墳頂部 7個 ** 墳丘中位平坦面の後円部北側 6個 ** 北くびれ部 7個 ** 前方部北側 6個 ** 墳丘南裾 3個 * 破片資料 ** 普通円筒埴輪 ** 朝顔形埴輪 ** 形象埴輪 - 蓋形、盾形など。 ; 土器類 * 石室前庭部出土 ** 須恵器 - 杯蓋1点。 ** 土師器 - 高杯1点。 * 墳丘各所出土 ** 須恵器 - 杯蓋、杯、無蓋高杯、高杯、ハソウ、壺、甕、器台。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 物集女車塚古墳（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち） *: 2016年（平成28年）3月1日、既指定の史跡「[[天皇の杜古墳]]」・「[[恵解山古墳]]」・「[[寺戸大塚古墳]]」の3基に物集女車塚古墳など8基を追加指定、「乙訓古墳群」に名称変更{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1638|乙訓古墳群}}。 === 京都府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 物集女車塚古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。向日文化財収蔵庫保管。2018年（平成30年）3月23日指定。 {{hidden begin |title = 京都府指定有形文化財「物集女車塚古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }}'''横穴式石室出土品''' * 金銅冠残欠 1頭分 * 玉類 ** 銀空玉 残欠共 49点 ** ガラス小玉 残欠共 1396点 ** ガラストンボ玉 残欠共 17点 ** ガラス管玉 3点 ** 碧玉棗玉 残欠共 2点 ** 琥珀棗玉 4点 ** 滑石臼玉 1点 * 銀耳環 2点 * 刀剣類 ** 鉄大刀残欠 一括 ** 鉄地銀張捩環頭大刀柄頭残欠 1点 ** 金銅三輪玉残欠 1点 ** 鹿角刀装具残欠 2点 ** 銀刀装具残欠 9点 ** 鉄鞘金具 1点 ** 鉄小刀 残欠共 一括 ** 鉄矛 3口 ** 鉄石突 3点 * 鉄鏃 残欠共 336点 * 工具類 ** 鉄刀子 鹿角把・残欠共 一括 * 馬具類 ** 鉄地金銅張f字形鏡板付轡残欠 1具分 ** 鉄環状鏡板付轡 1具 ** 鉄形式不明轡残欠 一括 ** 鉄地金銅張剣菱形杏葉 3点 ** 鉄地金銅張楕円形杏葉 1点 ** 鉄地金銅張形式不明杏葉残欠 一括 ** 鉄地金銅張磯金具 1組 ** 鉄地金銅張鞖金具 9点 ** 鉄地銀張飾鋲 42点 ** 青銅馬鐸 残欠共 3点 ** 鉄地金銅張有脚伏鉢形雲珠 1点 ** 鉄環状雲珠 2点 ** 鉄環状雲珠付属革金具・責金具 8点 ** 鉄地金銅張有脚伏鉢形辻金具 6点 ** 鉄地金銅張革金具組合せ式辻金具 8点 ** 鉄環状辻金具 1点 ** 鉄地金銅張革金具 11点 ** 鉄革金具 4点 ** 鉄鉸具 5点 * 土器類 ** 須恵器 残欠共 89点 ** 土師器 2点 * 不明鉄地金銅張金具残欠 6点 * その他鉄製品 ** 不明板状・帯状鉄製品 3点 ** 鉄釘 1点 ** 鉄鎹 1点 ** 紐状製品残欠 1点 '''墳丘出土品''' * 埴輪 残欠共 一括 * 土器類 ** 須恵器 残欠共 一括 ** 土師器 1点 * 銀耳環 1点 {{hidden end}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[物集女車塚古墳]] to [[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 物集女車塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
{{small|（右に前方部、左奥に後円部。右端は物集女街道）}} |別名 = 寺戸車塚古墳 |所属 = 乙訓古墳群 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[向日市]][[物集女|物集女町]]南条 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 57|緯度秒 = 28.80 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 41|経度秒 = 57.50 |ISO = JP-26 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長46m（48m）
高さ9m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式家形石棺） |出土品 = 金銅広帯式冠・馬具ほか副葬品多数、[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「物集女車塚古墳」
（「乙訓古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財 = 出土品（京都府指定文化財） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北東 |ラベル = 物集女
車塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = right }} '''物集女車塚古墳'''（もずめくるまづかこふん）は、[[京都府]][[向日市]][[物集女|物集女町]]南条にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。乙訓古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）、出土品は京都府指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+国の史跡「乙訓古墳群」13基 !期!!古墳名!!形状!!規模!!埋葬施設 |- |rowspan=2|1||[[五塚原古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長91m|| |- |[[元稲荷古墳]]||[[前方後方墳]]||墳丘長94m||竪穴式石槨 |- |2||[[寺戸大塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長98m||竪穴式石槨2 |- |3||[[長法寺南原古墳]]||前方後方墳||墳丘長60m||竪穴式石槨
石棺系小石室 |- |rowspan=2|4||[[天皇の杜古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m|| |- |[[鳥居前古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長51m||竪穴式石槨 |- |5||[[恵解山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長128m||竪穴式石槨?
副葬品埋納施設 |- |7||南条古墳||[[円墳]]||直径23.5m|| |- |rowspan=4|9||芝古墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長32.7m|| |- |井ノ内車塚古墳||前方後円墳||墳丘長37m|| |- |'''物集女車塚古墳'''||前方後円墳||墳丘長48m||横穴式石室 |- |[[井ノ内稲荷塚古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長46m||横穴式石室
木棺直葬 |- |11||今里大塚古墳||円墳||直径45m||横穴式石室 |}
[[京都盆地]]南西縁、向日丘陵北東部の緩傾斜面上（標高21メートル）に築造された古墳である。かつて[[淳和天皇]]の霊柩車を埋納したとする伝承があり、「車塚」の古墳名はこれに由来する。現在は東側に[[京都府道・大阪府道67号西京高槻線|府道67号西京高槻線]]（物集女街道）が通り、その敷設の際に前方部の一部が削平されているほか、[[1983年]]（[[昭和]]58年）以降に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北東方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]列のほか、[[葺石]]が認められる。墳丘南側くびれ部には[[造出]]を付す。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる。埋葬施設は後円部における片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南南東方向に開口する{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。石室全長11メートルを測る大型石室であり、[[ヤマト王権]]下で規格化された「畿内型」石室の特徴を呈する。石室内には組合式家形石棺が据えられ、石棺内外からは広帯式冠・耳環・鈴などの装身具類、鹿角製捩環頭大刀・銀製刀装具・大刀・鉄鉾などの武器類、馬具類、[[須恵器]]などの多量の副葬品が検出されている{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]中葉頃（TK10新相型式期）と推定され、[[7世紀]]初頭頃まで（TK43古相型式期・TK209型式期）3人の追葬が推測される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。乙訓地域を代表する盟主的首長墓として位置づけられ、規格化された畿内型石室を採用するとともに、質・量とも優れた副葬品を有する点で特筆される古墳になる。当時の[[ヤマト王権]]は[[継体天皇|継体大王]]の時代であり、[[市尾墓山古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]などの大臣・大連級古墳の次階層の武人的性格の人物の墓と想定され、継体王権における政治階層の墓制秩序の実態を知るうえで注目されるほか、継体大王が置いたとされる[[弟国宮]]との関連性が指摘される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 古墳域は[[2016年]]（[[平成]]28年）3月1日に国の[[史跡]]に指定され（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）、出土品は[[2018年]]（平成30年）に京都府指定有形文化財に指定された。現在では史跡整備のうえで公開されているが、石室内への立ち入りは制限され、毎年春にのみ一般公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]、『山州名跡志』・『[[山陵志]]』に[[淳和天皇]]の車の埋納塚として記述{{Sfn|物集女車塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 * [[1926年]]（[[昭和]]元年）、道路敷設（現在の[[京都府道・大阪府道67号西京高槻線|府道67号西京高槻線]]）に伴う前方部の一部削平。 * [[1931年]]（昭和6年）、墳丘測量調査：第1次調査（[[梅原末治]]）{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * 昭和50年頃には、前方部の崩壊が約半分まで進行{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）以降、発掘調査（向日市教育委員会）。 ** 第2次調査：墳丘確認調査。 ** 第3次調査：石室内部調査、墳丘くびれ部補足調査。 ** 第4次調査：墳形・造出確認調査。 ** 第5次調査：石室羨道部の解体調査。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）４月14日、京都府指定史跡に指定。 * [[1992年|1992]]-[[1994年]]度（[[平成]]4-6年度）、史跡整備。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）3月1日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「乙訓古墳群」のうち）。 * [[2018年]]（平成30年）3月23日、出土品が京都府指定有形文化財に指定。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|墳丘}}{{small|右に後円部、左奥に前方部。}}]] 古墳の規模は次の通り[https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/rekimachi/rekishi_bunkazai/bunkazai/kofun_iseki/kofun/1441755803803.html 物集女車塚古墳（古墳・遺跡 ）]（向日市ホームページ）。。 * 墳丘長：46メートル（資料によっては48メートル{{Sfn|京都の文化財 第36集|2019}}） * 後円部 - 2段築成。 ** 直径：24-32メートル ** 高さ：9メートル * 前方部 - 2段築成。 ** 長さ：18-23メートル ** 幅：39メートル ** 高さ：8メートル 墳丘は左右非対称の前方後円形で、北側では前方部が直線的で前面が大きく開くが、南側では緩くくびれて側面に造出を付す{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。葺石は後円部・くびれ部の墳丘上段裾部にのみ、鉢巻状に施される{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。埴輪列は、墳丘平坦面のくびれ部付近・墳頂部、南側造出上にのみ配列されたと見られる。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡る可能性が高く、北側には外堤状施設が存在した可能性がある{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 埋葬施設としては、後円部において片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 * 石室全長：約11メートル * 玄室：長さ5.1メートル、幅2.6メートル、高さ3.0メートル * 羨道：長さ5.8メートル、幅1.2-1.5メートル、高さ1.7メートル 玄室では、側壁は5段積み、前壁は3段積みで、天井石は4枚。袖部は2段積みである。羨道部の作業単位は、墳丘構築時の第一次墳丘と一致しており、墳丘・石室が連動して構築された様子が示唆される。石室の床面には排水溝が構築される。閉塞は前庭部埋土・閉塞石・集積遺構からなり、完存していたため3回の追葬が確認されている{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 石室内の玄室奥には、凝灰岩製組合式家形石棺が据えられる。石棺は石室主軸に直交し、内法で長辺1.86メートル・短辺0.88メートル・高さ0.74メートルを測る。底石3枚・長側石2枚・短側石2枚・蓋石3枚からなり、長側石以外の各石材の側面には縄掛突起を付す。また石棺の内外面には赤色顔料が施される。この石棺の蓋石上には、凝灰岩7石を組合せた棚状構造物が存在したと見られるほか、玄室床面からは石棺内のものと見られる石枕が出土している{{Sfn|乙訓古墳群調査報告書|2015}}。 現在は、石室のうち羨道部は解体復元されている。 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sekkan.jpg|玄室の組合式家形石棺 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, entrance-1.jpg|石室入り口 Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|石室排水溝（上に石室入り口） == 出土品 == === 石室出土 === [[ファイル:物集女車塚古墳出土 玉類・須恵器 Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)

(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|寺戸大塚古墳|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|ja|天皇の杜古墳|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Egayama Kofun|ja|恵解山古墳|1=Igenoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egayama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Igenoyama Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=[[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |[[:en:Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |[[:en:Igenoyama Kofun|Igenoyama Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=[[File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
{{small|(Right: anterior portion, left background: posterior circular portion. The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |[[:en:Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|Tenno-no-Mori Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |[[:en:Igenoyama Kofun|Igenoyama Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical [[haniwa]] rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the [[Yamato court]]. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with [[Sue pottery]] pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the [[Yamato court]] during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

The Mozume Road is visible at the far right.)}} |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster |location=[[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Muko]], Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)

(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, [[Sue pottery]], [[Haji pottery]], [[haniwa]] |Sub-tombs= |built=[[6th century]] mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National [[Historic site]]: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture [[Tangible designation1]]. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |[[:en:Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun]]||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
