Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Sub-tombs= |built={{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}}: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123116949}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber

Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} |Sub-tombs= |built={{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}} mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site}}: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123116949}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|WD=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Igenoyama Kofun|en|Igenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11492427|lt=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Sub-tombs= |built={{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}}: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|WD=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chohoujiminamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123116949}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|WD=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Igenoyama Kofun|en|Igenoyama Kofun|WD=Q11492427|lt=Igenoyama Kofun|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==

Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Sub-tombs= |built={{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}}: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123116949}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|qid=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Igenoyama Kofun|en|Igenoyama Kofun|qid=Q11492427|lt=Igenoyama Kofun|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[en:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[en:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] [[en:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]][[ja:物集女車塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] |name= Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun |image=File:Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg |alternate_name= Terado Kurumazuka Kofun | part_of= {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qid=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} |location={{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Muko|en|Muko|qid=Q1952026|lt=Muko|1=Muko}}, Mozume Town, Minami |coordinates={{coord|34|57|28.80|N|135|41|57.50|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type=[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |area=Mound length: 46 m (48 m)
Height: 9 m (posterior circular portion) |burial= Single-shouldered [[horizontal stone chamber]]
(with an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus) |burial goods= Gilt-bronze crown, horse fittings, numerous grave goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Sub-tombs= |built={{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} mid-period |Buried individual= |Imperial tomb= |Historic Site= National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site|1=Historic site}}: Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun
(Included in the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster") |Tangible cultural property= excavations (Kyoto Prefecture-Historic site) |notes= |map= Japan Kyoto city |type= Keyhole-shaped Kofun-NE |map_dot_label= Mozume
Kurumazuka Kofun |map_dot_label= right }} '''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun''' (物集女車塚古墳) is a [[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]] located in Mozume Town, Muko City, Kyoto Prefecture. It is one of the Kofuns that constitute the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster. Designated as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster"), its excavated burial goods have been Historic site as a Kyoto Prefecture Historical Site. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+ National Historic site: "Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster" (13 mounds) !built !name of Kofun!!type!!area!!burial |- |rowspan=2|1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Itsukahara Kofun|lt=Gotzukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gotzukahara Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 91 m|| |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|lt=Motoinari Kofun|lt=Moto-Inari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Inari Kofun|qq=}}||[[:en:Keyhole-shaped kofun|Keyhole-shaped kofun]]||Mound length: 94 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 98 m||Vertical stone burial chambers: 2 |- |3 |{{ill|Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|ja|長法寺南原古墳|1=Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōhōji Minamihara Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123116949}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 60 m||Vertical stone burial chamber
Small stone chamber type |- |rowspan=2|4 |{{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|qid=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 83 m|| |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|lt=Toriimae Kofun|lt=Torii-mae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii-mae Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 51 m||Vertical stone burial chamber |- |5 |{{ill|Igenoyama Kofun|en|Igenoyama Kofun|qid=Q11492427|lt=Igenoyama Kofun|1=Igenoyama Kofun}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 128 m||Vertical stone burial chamber?
Grave goods burial facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun||[[Round Kofun]]||Diameter: 23.5 m|| |- |rowspan=4|9 |Shiba Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 32.7 m|| |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 37 m|| |- |'''Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun'''||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 48 m||Horizontal stone chamber |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}}||Keyhole-shaped Kofun||Mound length: 46 m||Horizontal stone chamber
Wooden coffin inhumation |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun||Round Kofun||Diameter: 45 m||Horizontal stone chamber |}
This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. Currently, the [[Kyoto Prefecture Route 67|Route 67 Kyoto-Takatsuki Line]] (Mozume Road) passes east of the mound, having partially destroyed the anterior portion. Since 1983, several excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped with the anterior portion oriented northeast. It is constructed in two tiers. Surface features include cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} rows and [[fukiishi]] paving stones. A surrounding moat encircles the mound, with a constricted neck area featuring an attached projection. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}. Inside the chamber is an assembled house-shaped sarcophagus. Grave goods found include a gilt-bronze crown, earrings, beads, weapons such as swords and spears, and horse fittings, along with {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} The kofun dates to the mid-6th century (TK10 phase), with evidence suggesting three subsequent burials by the early 7th century (TK43 and TK209 phases). It is regarded as a representative kofun of the Oyamazaki region and as the burial site of a military leader associated with the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}} during the reign of Emperor Keitai. Scholars also link it to the political structure of the court and its relations with the Otogokumiyazono palace.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} In 2016, the kofun was Historic site as a [[National Historic site]] (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster), and its burial goods were Historic site as Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 in 2018. Public access is available after preservation efforts; however, entry into the burial chamber is restricted to a spring open house event. == burial goods == === Chamber Finds === * Gilt-bronze crown (unique example within Kyoto Prefecture) * Beads (glass, jade, amber, silver) * Weapons (iron swords, spears, arrowheads, armor components) * Horse fittings (gilded and iron pieces) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) === Mound Finds === * Haniwa fragments (cylindrical, morning glory-shaped) * Pottery (Sue pottery, Haji pottery) == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun (part of the Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster) *: Designated on March 1, 2016.{{designation1 Database|401|1638|Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster}} === Kyoto Prefecture Tangible designation1 === * burial goods from Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun: Designated March 23, 2018. Stored at Muko designation1 Repository. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|publisher=Muko City Board of Education|year=2015}} * {{Cite book|title=Kyoto Historic site Vol. 36|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2019}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun}} * [https://www.city.muko.kyoto.jp/ Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun - Muko City] [[Igenoyama Kofun]] ...fun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc ... 825 bytes (86 words) - 21:01, 15 January Nagahouji-Nambahara Kofun |{{ill|Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|lt=Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} ... 8 KB (1,031 words) - 20:59, 15 January List of Historic sites of Japan (Kyoto) ...[Nanjō Kofun]]|南条古墳}}, {{nihongo|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]|[[:ja:物集女車塚古墳|物集女車塚古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Igenoyama Kofun]]|[[:ja:恵解山古墳|恵解山古墳]]}}, {{nihongo|[[Inouc | align="center"|[[Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun]]

This kofun was constructed on the gently sloping northeastern area of the Mukoyama Hills, southwest of the [[Kyoto Basin]], at an elevation of 21 meters. According to legend, it housed the funeral carriage of Emperor Junna, from which the name "Kurumazuka" derives. The burial chamber, located in the posterior circular portion, is a single-shouldered horizontal stone chamber opening to the south-southeast.{{Cite book|title=Oyamazaki Kofun Cluster Survey Report|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture Board of Education|year=2015}} Measuring 11 meters in total length, it exhibits features characteristic of "Kinai-style" chambers standardized under the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship|1=Yamato Kingship}}.
